metadata.program_name: Informativ Tier Certification — Protect | Enrich | Elevate | Control
metadata.description: 4-module certification teaching reps to differentiate Informativ's four package tiers, map customer pain to the right tier, and articulate tier-specific value. Built from the Value Selling Training Outline, Tiered Product Story, and Feature-Advantage-Benefit framework.

modules.title: Protect — Compliance-First Credit Access
modules.description: The baseline tier. Every customer enters the platform on Protect. This module covers what Protect includes, who it's built for, and how to position compliance as the floor — not the ceiling.
key_concepts: Protect is the mandatory starting point — no customer enters without compliance embedded
Core features: FCRA, OFAC, Red Flags, Privacy Notices, credit report access, adverse action automation, audit-ready deal documentation
Value message: Every credit pull is compliant, every deal is protected from day one
Who it's for: Every dealership — this is the baseline
Protect starts at 75 vehicles sold per month
Never position credit-only — always lead with compliance as the floor
Key value driver: Stay Safe — protect from regulatory, legal, and reputational risk

questions.question: What is the primary positioning of the Protect tier?

options.B: Compliance-first credit access — every customer starts here
options.C: A premium enterprise solution
options.D: A fraud detection package

questions.question: True or False: Some customers can enter the Informativ platform without the compliance baseline included in Protect.

options.B: False

questions.question: Which compliance components are included in Protect?

options.A: FCRA, OFAC, Red Flags, and Privacy Notices
options.B: Only OFAC and Red Flags
options.C: Only credit report access
options.D: Fraud detection and identity scanning

questions.question: What is the value message for Protect?

options.B: Stronger fraud defense without workflow friction
options.C: Every credit pull is compliant, every deal is protected from day one
options.D: Consistency, compliance, and speed in one automated flow

questions.question: What is the minimum vehicle threshold where Protect is typically positioned?

options.B: 50 vehicles/month
options.C: 75 vehicles/month
options.D: 100 vehicles/month

questions.question: When positioning Informativ to a prospect, what should a rep always lead with?

options.B: SmartPencil features
options.C: Compliance as the floor — then sell up based on discovered pain
options.D: The full Control package

questions.question: Which of Informativ's three primary value drivers is most aligned with Protect?

options.A: Make More — increase revenue per vehicle
options.B: Save More — reduce fraud losses and operational waste
options.C: Stay Safe — protect from regulatory, legal, and reputational risk
options.D: All three equally

questions.question: What automation does Protect include for adverse action?

options.B: Automated adverse action notices
options.C: Only email notifications
options.D: Adverse action is only in Control

questions.question: A dealer says they only need credit reports. How should a rep respond?

options.A: Decline the deal — Informativ doesn't sell credit-only packages
options.B: Sell them credit access and move on
options.C: Acknowledge where they are today, sell them Credit stand alone, and let them know their needs may change over time — Informativ can grow with them as their business evolves
options.D: Recommend they use a competitor until they need more features

questions.question: What does audit-ready deal documentation mean in the context of Protect?

options.A: Documents are printed and filed manually
options.B: Every deal has compliant, defensible documentation stored digitally and ready for regulatory review
options.C: Audits are handled by Informativ's legal team
options.D: Documentation is optional

modules.title: Enrich — Fraud Defense + Enhanced Credit Intelligence
modules.description: The step up from Protect for dealers who need fraud defense and compliance but are new to Informativ and not quite ready for soft-pull-only with Elevate. Covers Enrich-specific features, the Enrich credit display difference (no visible credit score), SmartPencil introduction, and how to identify dealers who fit the Enrich profile.
key_concepts: Enrich is for dealers who need fraud defense and compliance but are new to Informativ and not quite ready for soft-pull-only with Elevate
Ideal for dealers experiencing slowdown at the desk who want a robust offering but face sticker shock when they see the price of Control (hard pulls and soft pulls combined)
Value message: Compliance, fraud defense, and faster throughput at the desk without added workflow friction
Multi-layer fraud detection: ID scanning, synthetic ID checks, Out-of-Wallet, MLA
Key difference: In Enrich, the credit score and bureau are NOT shown to the desk manager
The system still uses credit data to calculate payments — it is just hidden from the user. Dealers see lender programs the consumer qualifies for in SmartPencil
SmartPencil is the first tier that includes SmartPencil for deal structuring
100 vehicles/month is the threshold where Enrich or higher becomes appropriate
Value drivers: All three — Stay Safe, Save More, and Make More

questions.question: Who is the Enrich tier built for?

options.A: Dealers who only need baseline credit and compliance coverage at the lowest cost
options.B: Dealers who need fraud and compliance but are new to Informativ and not quite ready for soft-pull-only with Elevate
options.C: Enterprise dealer groups with 500+ vehicles per month who need custom integrations
options.D: Dealers who are already on Elevate but want to add hard pull credit reports back into their workflow

questions.question: What is unique about how credit data is displayed in Enrich compared to Elevate and Control?

options.B: Credit score and bureau are NOT shown to the desk manager in Enrich
options.C: Enrich does not use credit data at all
options.D: There is no difference

questions.question: True or False: In Enrich, the system still uses credit data to calculate SmartPencil payments even though the score is hidden.

options.B: False

questions.question: After the prequal consent form is completed in Enrich, where does the dealership see the soft pull credit information?

options.A: The desk manager reviews the full soft pull credit report and score
options.B: The credit data is emailed to the F&I manager for review
options.C: They go to SmartPencil, where they don't see the score or report but do see the lender programs the consumer qualifies for
options.D: The soft pull results are displayed in the compliance dashboard

questions.question: Which fraud detection layers are included in Enrich?

options.B: ID scanning, synthetic ID checks, Out-of-Wallet, and MLA
options.C: Only driver's license scanning
options.D: Red Flags and OFAC only

questions.question: What is the value message for Enrich?

options.A: Every credit pull is compliant from day one
options.B: Enterprise-grade compliance and revenue acceleration
options.C: Compliance, fraud defense, and faster throughput at the desk without added workflow friction
options.D: Consistency, compliance, and speed in one automated flow

questions.question: At what vehicle volume threshold does Enrich or higher become the appropriate package fit?

options.B: 75 vehicles/month
options.C: 100 vehicles/month
options.D: 200 vehicles/month

questions.question: What is the first Informativ tier that includes SmartPencil?

options.A: Protect — SmartPencil is included in every tier as part of the baseline
options.B: Enrich — the first tier that introduces SmartPencil for deal structuring
options.C: Elevate — SmartPencil is only available with soft-pull-only packages
options.D: Control — SmartPencil requires both hard pull and soft pull to function

questions.question: What is the primary feature Enrich adds beyond Protect?

options.A: Full credit report and score visibility for the desk manager
options.B: SmartPencil — enabling deal structuring with lender programs the consumer qualifies for
options.C: Automated CRM integration and lead scoring capabilities
options.D: Direct lender submission and deal push functionality

questions.question: Which value driver is most aligned with Enrich?

options.A: Make More — increase revenue per vehicle
options.B: Save More — reduce fraud losses and operational waste
options.C: Stay Safe — regulatory protection only
options.D: All three — Stay Safe, Save More, and Make More

modules.title: Elevate — Compliance Automation + Operational Efficiency
modules.description: Elevate is for dealers willing to go soft-pull-only who require all the features Informativ has to offer. Essentially the same as Control minus the hard pull baseline. A great fit for current Control customers impacted by credit bureau price increases, and for dealers ready to adopt the soft-pull-only model.
key_concepts: Elevate is built for dealers willing to go soft-pull-only who require all the features Informativ has to offer
Great package for current Control customers to downgrade to when credit bureau price increases impact them
Value message: Full access to every Informativ feature with reduced compliance risk by moving to soft pulls only
Elevate includes everything — all three value drivers: Stay Safe, Save More, and Make More
In Elevate, the desk manager CAN see the customer's credit score and bureau information
The Risk Based Pricing Notice is generated off the soft pull, unlike other packages where it comes off the hard pull
Credit Guardrails put control in the dealer's hands on when, how often, and how much credit is utilized in the showroom
Hard pulls can still be offered transactionally in Elevate
Elevate is essentially the same as Control except it is soft-pull-only
eZ Form Fill automates form completion to reduce manual data entry

questions.question: Who is the Elevate tier built for?

options.A: Every dealership as a baseline compliance solution
options.B: Dealers needing only fraud protection without SmartPencil
options.C: Dealers willing to go soft-pull-only who require all the features Informativ has to offer
options.D: Enterprise groups with 500+ vehicles requiring custom integrations

questions.question: What is the value message for Elevate?

options.A: Every credit pull is compliant from day one
options.B: Full access to every Informativ feature with reduced compliance risk by moving to soft pulls only
options.C: Stronger fraud defense without workflow friction
options.D: Enterprise-grade revenue acceleration with hard pull and soft pull combined

questions.question: Which feature automates form completion to reduce manual data entry?

options.B: Credit Guardrails
options.C: eZ Form Fill
options.D: Digital Deal Jacket

questions.question: True or False: In Elevate, the desk manager CAN see the customer's credit score and bureau information.

options.B: False

questions.question: What do Credit Guardrails do in the showroom?

options.A: Block all credit pulls from being processed
options.B: Put control in the dealer's hands on when, how often, and how much credit is utilized in the showroom
options.C: Generate SmartPencil payments automatically
options.D: Manage lender submissions and deal routing

questions.question: In Elevate, how is the Risk Based Pricing Notice generated differently than in other packages?

options.A: The Risk Based Pricing Notice is not required in Elevate
options.B: It is generated off the soft pull, whereas in other packages it is generated off the hard pull
options.C: It is generated manually by the F&I manager after deal submission
options.D: It is generated off the hard pull, same as every other package

questions.question: What is the primary difference between Elevate and Control?

options.A: Elevate includes more features than Control
options.B: Control is soft-pull-only while Elevate uses hard pulls
options.C: Elevate is soft-pull-only while Control includes both hard pull and soft pull
options.D: Elevate does not include Customer Insights while Control does

questions.question: True or False: In Elevate, hard pulls can still be offered transactionally.

options.B: False

questions.question: How does Elevate differ from Enrich on credit visibility?

options.A: Both hide the soft pull credit score from the desk manager
options.B: Elevate shows the soft pull credit score and bureau to desk managers; Enrich hides them
options.C: Enrich shows more credit detail than Elevate
options.D: There is no difference in credit visibility between the two

questions.question: Which value driver combination best describes Elevate?

options.B: Save More + Stay Safe — operational efficiency and compliance automation
options.C: Stay Safe only
options.D: Make More + Save More + Stay Safe equally

modules.title: Control — Full-Platform Optimization + Revenue Acceleration
modules.description: The enterprise tier. Full-funnel automation, SmartPencil prequalification, income and employment verification, customer insights, complete fraud suite, and digital retailing optimization. Covers how to position Control for enterprise groups and high-volume rooftops.
key_concepts: Built for enterprise groups and high-volume rooftops seeking full-funnel automation and marketing ROI
Value message: Enterprise-grade compliance, funding speed, and customer intelligence that drives revenue
Includes everything in Protect + Enrich + Elevate PLUS:
Soft-pull prequalification (SmartPencil)
Income and employment verification
Customer insights and lead scoring
Complete fraud suite
Full compliance guardrails
Digital retailing optimization
Legacy name: Total Solution maps to Control
Hits all three value drivers: Make More + Save More + Stay Safe

questions.question: Who is the Control tier built for?

options.B: Enterprise groups and high-volume rooftops seeking full-funnel automation and marketing ROI
options.C: Dealers who only need compliance
options.D: Dealers with fewer than 75 vehicles/month

questions.question: What is the value message for Control?

options.B: Stronger fraud defense without friction
options.C: Enterprise-grade compliance, funding speed, and customer intelligence that drives revenue
options.D: Consistency and speed in one automated flow

questions.question: Which feature is unique to Control and not available in lower tiers?

options.B: Customer Insights for marketing and retention
options.C: Full access to both hard pulls and soft pulls together
options.D: Fraud detection and synthetic ID checks

questions.question: What legacy tier name maps to Control?

options.B: Enrich
options.C: Total Solution
options.D: Elevate

questions.question: A Control customer says their pricing is now too high due to credit bureau cost increases. What is the ideal package to move them to without compromising features?

options.A: Protect — the lowest cost entry point to reduce their spend immediately
options.B: Enrich — removes the soft pull to cut costs while keeping fraud protection
options.C: Elevate — delivers every Informativ feature at a lower cost by moving to soft-pull-only
options.D: There is no alternative — Control is the only tier with full feature access

questions.question: If you move a customer from Control to a less expensive package, that opportunity is considered what?

options.A: New — full quota and booking credit
options.B: Cross-Sell — full quota and booking credit
options.C: Reprice — quota credit but no booking credit
options.D: Downgrade — no quota or booking credit

questions.question: Which of Informativ's three value drivers does Control deliver on?

options.B: Save More only
options.C: Stay Safe only
options.D: All three: Make More, Save More, and Stay Safe

questions.question: A 15-rooftop enterprise group wants full-funnel automation, prequalification, customer intelligence, and traditional hard pull credit access. Which tier fits?

options.B: Enrich
options.C: Elevate
options.D: Control

questions.question: What is the main value add that Control provides vs all other packages?

options.A: Control is the only package with SmartPencil and fraud detection
options.B: Control is the only package that blends traditional credit access with the full Informativ feature set and Credit Guardrails
options.C: Control is the only package that includes compliance automation
options.D: Control is the only package available for multi-rooftop dealer groups

questions.question: A dealer that sells 250 vehicles a month is on Protect. They are acquired by an enterprise-level auto group that needs more robust features and is unfamiliar with soft pulls. What should the rep recommend?

options.A: Stay on Protect — it covers everything they need at their volume
options.B: Enrich — adds fraud protection and SmartPencil at a lower cost than Control
options.C: Elevate — gives them full features with soft-pull-only access
options.D: Control — full feature set with traditional hard pull credit access the group is familiar with

questions.question: How does digital retailing optimization in Control benefit the dealer?

options.B: It optimizes the online-to-showroom customer journey with prequalification and lead intelligence
options.C: It automates vehicle pricing
options.D: It manages social media

questions.question: When should a rep use the Compliance Risk and Opportunity Audit to help steer a customer toward Control?

options.B: Only after they sign a contract
options.C: During discovery — to surface compliance gaps, fraud exposure, and operational inefficiencies that justify the full platform
options.D: Only if the competitor mentions it
